title: LSH-SFA for Autoregressive Generation: Causal Mode and Conversation Ability
description: We present the application of LSH-SFA to autoregressive language generation with causal mode. The key design is direction bias on wave vector k: k[i] += bias × (i / seq_len), ensuring future tokens have larger k values and are naturally attenuated by Gaussian similarity. For generation tasks, we replace the diffusion equation with the convection equation (∂F/∂t = -c·∂F/∂x + βF³) which provides hard causal propagation from left to right. We implement KV cache for efficient autoregressive decoding, reducing per-step complexity from O(n²) to O(n). Experiments on dialogue generation, story completion, and code synthesis demonstrate that LSH-SFA's causal mode achieves comparable perplexity to Transformer baselines while maintaining natural causality through PDE evolution rather than attention masking.
